titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Meryle Secrest Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday May 14, 2019 at 03:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 21-24 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 25 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Meryle Secrest and Lion Heart Autographs, Inc. on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2015.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Writings and Research, 1970-2011. II. Correspondence, 1900-2009. III. Photographs and Research, 1900-2011. IV. Index Cards and Subject Files, 1970-2005. V. Interviews, 1990-2008. VI. Electronic Media, circa 2000-2009.

bioghist.head: Meryle Secrest
bioghist.p: Meryle Secrest is a journalist and biographyer. Born in Bath, England, she emigrated to Canada with her family, and eventually to the United States, where she worked for The Washington Post from 1964 to 1975. Secrest left The Washington Post to focus on writing books, and has published a number of biographies, including Being Bernard Berenson (Harmondsworth, 1980), which was a finalist for the 1980 Pulitzer Prize in Biography. In 2006 she received the National Humanities Medal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Meryle Secrest papers, which span from 1900 to 2011, contain writings, notes, correspondence, research materials, and audio recordings related to the life, work, and primarily the writings, of Meryle Secrest.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Meryle Secrest Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Meryle Secrest Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts, notes, clippings, primarily related to research interviews performed by Secrest.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include chapter notes and changes.

arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence related to Secrest's life and work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence includes copies of Frank Lloyd Wright's business and family correspondence as well as correspondence generated by Secrest while doing research for her book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include correspondence between Secrest and Sondhiem and other correspondence pertaining to Secrest's biograpy of Sondheim.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Secrest received while doing research for her biography on Rodgers.

did.unittitle: Berenson family correspondence and other materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include autographs, along with personal correspondence and correspondence from and to others. Autographs with correspondence included are Jean Hugo, Barbara Cook, David Cornwell (John le Carré), Benjamin Spock M.D., Roddy McDowall, William Coldstream, Katerina Wilczynski, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Secrest received while doing research for her biography on Berenson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains audio recordings of interviews performed during Secrest's biographical research projects.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
